mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and supports higher stock prices over the near term. The signal is always active and positive for forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window or positive returns do not exceed baseline in periods of rising RSAFS (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 2.0% from a low level, the monetary tightening cycle begins, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative returns in less than 60% of triggered cases over a 3-year period, or average forward return is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: High CPI inflation combined with a low federal funds rate signals that real interest rates are deeply negative, prompting expectations of future monetary tightening that weighs on stock prices negatively.
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return is positive or statistically insignificant (t-test p > 0.10) in periods meeting the trigger, or Rank IC < 0.0 over a 3-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is low (≤4.0%) and industrial production is high (≥110), the economy is in a late-cycle expansion where further positive surprises are limited, leading to below-average forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward return during the regime is non-negative or exceeds the unconditional mean by more than 1 standard error over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) rising indicates expanding real economic activity, which supports corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, or the top decile of INDPRO growth months do not outperform the bottom decile by at least 2% annualized over the forward 60-day window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) is a clear recession indicator that immediately reprices equity risk premiums higher, producing negative short-term forward returns.
failure_condition: After a PAYEMS decline, 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances or the average return is not statistically less than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When both retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) are above their respective six-month moving averages, the economy exhibits synchronized demand and supply strength, which is bullish for stocks over a short horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS above 6-month MA and INDPRO above 6-month MA
failure_condition: The average 5-day return during the joint condition is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's accuracy rate is below 55% over any 2-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 2.0% and CPI inflation is above 250 (index level), real interest rates are negative, which encourages borrowing and investment, boosting equity prices over the near term.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return during the regime is not statistically positive (t-test p > 0.10), or the hit rate is below 50% over a 3-year rolling period.
